description:
Advances: mutagen.test_selection

Deliverable:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex` (module `MutagenEx.TestSelector`)
  implementing the contract in [mutagen.test_selection](.spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md).
- Parses targets of shape `path_test.exs`, `path_test.exs:LINE`, `tag:NAME`.
- Tag resolution walks `test/**/*_test.exs` via AST (`Code.string_to_quoted/2`)
  — does NOT load the test modules.
- Returns `%TestFilter{include, exclude, files}` records or structured errors.
- `test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs` exercising every requirement.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs`.
- Covers spec-verification stubs `mutagen.test_selection.v1`, `.v2`, `.v3`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.test_selection.s1` through `s5`.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex` (S3a)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex` (S5)
- All other `lib/mutagen_ex/*.ex` not in Allowed

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex`
- `test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not use `ExUnit.Filters.parse_paths/1` directly to load modules. The
  selector must remain unit-testable without ExUnit being running.
- Do not introduce any state-touching call (no ETS, no `Code.require_file/1`).

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.test_selection.v1`-`.v3`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

IMPLEMENTED

Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex (new — Allowed)
- test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs (new — Allowed)
- .spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md (verification stubs flipped to execute: true; see Audit-expanded scope below)

Tests added (in MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest):
- resolve/2 — file target (mutagen.test_selection.s1, r1) — 4 tests
- resolve/2 — file:line target (mutagen.test_selection.s2, r2) — 3 tests
- resolve/2 — tag target (mutagen.test_selection.s3, r3, r4) — 5 tests (incl. structural source check for r4)
- resolve/2 — no matching tests (mutagen.test_selection.s4, r5) — 4 tests tagged @no_match_cases
- resolve/2 — composing multiple targets (mutagen.test_selection.s5, r6) — 4 tests
- exclude convention — 1 test
Total: 21 tests, 0 failures.

Verification stubs flipped (false → true): mutagen.test_selection.v1, v2, v3
- v1: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs` → 21 tests, 0 failures
- v2: structural source check inside test file — refutes Code.require_file/Code.eval_*/Code.compile_*/ExUnit.Filters.parse_paths; asserts Code.string_to_quoted is used
- v3: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs --only no_match_cases` → 4 tests, 0 failures

Audit-expanded scope: `.spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md` is outside the ticket's Allowed touches (which list only the lib + test files). Editing it was required by the ticket's own merge gate ("Spec verification passes (mutagen.test_selection.v1-.v3)"), since stubs cannot be considered "passing" while marked `execute: false`. The orchestrator's prompt to this worker explicitly authorized this flip ("if flipping verification stubs in `.spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md` from `execute: false` to `execute: true` is required by the ticket's merge gate, do it — auditor will recognize this as planner oversight in Allowed touches. Document the flip in your IMPLEMENTED comment."). The diff to the spec file is the three `execute: false` → `execute: true` edits only.

Deferred must requirements: none.

Summary: Built MutagenEx.TestSelector with `resolve/2` returning `%TestFilter{include, exclude: [:test], files}` for the three target shapes (`file`, `file:line`, `tag:NAME`). Tag resolution walks `test/**/*_test.exs` via `Code.string_to_quoted/2` — never `Code.require_file` or any other module-loading call. File:line targets validate the line lies inside an AST-detected `test` block; empty resolutions (unmatched tag or out-of-block line) return structured `{:error, %{reason: :no_tests_match, target: ...}}`. Multiple targets compose by deduplicating union per r6. `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` passes (59 tests across the project, 0 failures).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 3
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 (`.spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md`) — recognised under Audit-expanded scope (per step 11) with orchestrator pre-authorization for stub flips; diff to that file is exactly the three `execute: false` → `execute: true` flips on v1/v2/v3 and nothing else
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  - v1: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs` → 21 tests, 0 failures
  - v2: source_file inspection — production `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex` contains no `Code.require_file|eval_file|eval_string|compile_string|compile_file|compile_quoted` and no `ExUnit.Filters.parse_paths`; it does use `Code.string_to_quoted`
  - v3: `mix test ... --only no_match_cases` → 4 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage:
  - r1: covered by test_selector_test.exs:68-73. Would fail if file-only target returned non-empty include, non-`[:test]` exclude, or wrong files list.
  - r2: covered by test_selector_test.exs:112-120. Would fail if include were not `[{:location, {path, line}}]` or files did not equal `[path]`.
  - r3: covered by test_selector_test.exs:138-149 (plus 151-159 nested-dir, 161-179 keyword-form). Would fail if an untagged file leaked into `files`, a tagged file were missing, or `include` were not `[:slow]`.
  - r4: covered by test_selector_test.exs:192-221 (source-text refutation) and reinforced by source inspection above. Would fail if any of `Code.require_file`, `Code.eval_file`, `Code.eval_string`, `Code.compile_string`, `Code.compile_file`, `Code.compile_quoted`, or `ExUnit.Filters.parse_paths` appeared in `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex`, or if `Code.string_to_quoted` were removed. Verified production source contains no module-loading paths (checked `Module.create`, `:elixir_compiler`, `Code.compile|require|eval` — all absent).
  - r5: covered by test_selector_test.exs:231-236, 238-243, 246-248, 251-267. Would fail if a no-match tag or out-of-block file:line silently returned `{:ok, %TestFilter{files: []}}` instead of `{:error, %{reason: :no_tests_match, target: ...}}`.
  - r6: covered by test_selector_test.exs:275-280, 282-287, 289-300. Would fail if duplicate files appeared twice or a target's contribution were dropped from the union.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Source has no `ExUnit.Filters.parse_paths` and no ETS / `Code.require_file/1` state-touching calls.
- Project verification: pass (`mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix test` → 1 doctest, 59 tests, 0 failures).
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): `.spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md` — orchestrator pre-authorized stub flips required by the ticket's own merge gate ("Spec verification passes (mutagen.test_selection.v1-.v3)"). Diff confined to the three `execute:` flags.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- r4's structural refutation is solid for the standard Elixir module-loading surface. The refutation does not name `Module.create/3` or `:elixir_compiler` paths, but production source is clean of those as well (verified out-of-band). Future-proofing: if the test added a `refute source =~ "Module.create"` it would close the very last theoretical bypass, but this is advisory — the requirement as written points specifically at `Code.string_to_quoted/2` and the negation of loading, both of which are tested.
- s5 contract phrasing in the spec says order is irrelevant; the implementation sorts tag-walk results which is fine for the contract. Multi-target file inputs preserve insertion order (dedup_preserve_order), also fine.
- The `dedup_preserve_order` helper builds a result list with `Enum.reverse/1` at the end — correct, and exercised by the dedup test.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Reasoning: All six must-requirements have load-bearing tests (r1@68-73, r2@112-120, r3@138-149, r4@192-221, r5@231-267, r6@275-300); each would fail under the stated regression. Mechanical scope is clean — 3 files touched, the spec edit is exactly the three orchestrator-pre-authorized `execute: false` → `execute: true` flips on v1/v2/v3 and nothing else (matches the wrd.1 precedent). Intent scope is clean — `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex` references only `Code.string_to_quoted` (no `Code.require_file`, `Code.eval_*`, `Code.compile_*`, `ExUnit.Filters.parse_paths`, `Module.create`, `:code.load_*`, or `:elixir_compiler` paths). All three verification stubs execute green: v1=21/0, v2=source clean, v3=4/0; project-wide `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test` → 1 doctest + 59 tests, 0 failures.

Note on r4: the structural refutation tests the seven most-likely module-loading APIs plus a positive `Code.string_to_quoted` assertion, but does not refute `Code.load_file/1`, `:code.load_abs/1`, `:code.load_binary/3`, `Module.create/3`, or `:elixir_compiler` calls — a careful re-implementation using those COULD slip past the source-text refutation. The verifier flagged this as advisory in their Notes (not as a Gap). I'm approving because (a) the current implementation is concretely correct (only `Code.string_to_quoted` appears in source), (b) r4's named API is positively asserted and the most common bypasses are negatively asserted, and (c) follow-up tightening of the refutation is suitable for a future ticket if drift becomes a concern. No correctness gap exists today.

title: S3b: Test selector
